body:
{
  "title": "Keel Networks",
  "compiled_truth": "Keel Networks is a cybersecurity startup founded in early 2026 by [Jin Castillo](people/jin-castillo), focused on zero-trust network access (ZTNA) and identity infrastructure. The company's thesis is straightforward: perimeter-based security is dead, and most existing ZTNA vendors bolt identity verification onto legacy network stacks rather than rethinking the data plane itself. Jin's background in distributed systems and protocol design gave Keel a technical edge from day one — the initial prototype demonstrated sub-millisecond policy evaluation at the network edge, which caught the attention of several early backers.

The seed round closed in Q2 2026, led by [Gil Papadopoulos](people/gil-papadopoulos) with participation from [Bex Andersen](people/bex-andersen) and [Dev Osei](people/dev-osei). Gil had been looking for a zero-trust play for nearly a year and reportedly wrote the term sheet within 48 hours of seeing a demo. Bex's involvement brought credibility in enterprise sales channels, which Keel needed badly given Jin's primarily technical background. Dev Osei came in slightly later in the round but has been an active advisor on go-to-market, particularly around positioning against incumbent ZTNA providers.

Keel's architecture treats identity as a first-class primitive in the network layer — every packet carries cryptographic attestation of both the sender and the policy context under which it was authorized. This is computationally expensive the naive way, but Keel has been exploring partnerships with deterministic data infrastructure providers to bring down verification overhead. There were conversations with Anchor Codec in late 2027 about integrating single-pass authentication primitives into Keel's edge nodes, though as of early 2028 no formal partnership has been anounced.

The team grew from 4 to about 22 people by mid-2028, mostly engineers with a small but effective sales team targeting mid-market enterprises. Keel landed its first six-figure contract with a logistics company in Q3 2027, and revenue has been growing steadily since. Jin has been vocal on the conference circuit about the failures of traditional VPN-based approaches, and the company's blog has become a suprisingly popular resource for security architects evaluating ZTNA alternatives.

The company operates out of a small office in Portland, Oregon, though roughly half the team is remote. Culture is described as intense but low-ego — a reflection of Jin's personality. Keel is expected to raise a Series A sometime in 2028.",
  "timeline": "- **2026-01-18** | Keel Networks incorporated in Oregon by [Jin Castillo](people/jin-castillo).
- **2026-04-03** | First working prototype of edge-native ZTNA agent demonstrated internally.
- **2026-06-12** | Seed round closed; [Gil Papadopoulos](people/gil-papadopoulos) leads, with [Bex Andersen](people/bex-andersen) and [Dev Osei](people/dev-osei) participating.
- **2026-09-22** | Hired first non-founding engineer, Mara Svensson, to lead the policy engine team.
- **2027-02-14** | Keel launches private beta with 11 design partners across logistics and fintech.
- **2027-07-09** | Jin presents "Identity at the Packet Level" talk at CipherCon; well received, generates inbound pipeline.
- **2027-09-30** | First six-figure annual contract signed with a mid-market logistics firm.
- **2027-11-15** | Exploratory meeting with Anchor Codec team to discuss integrating single-pass authentication primitives into Keel's edge verification stack.
- **2028-01-20** | Team reaches 22 people; opens second small office in Austin.
- **2028-04-08** | [Dev Osei](people/dev-osei) introduces Keel to two enterprise prospects through his network; both enter POC stage."
}
